title: Hello World
text:
This demo note shows how categories and the listing page work.



Each note specifies tags in its YAML front matter. These appear in Obsidian’s tag pane and are automatically converted to Quarto categories for the listing page.
tags: [demo, layout]



The home page (index.qmd) uses Quarto’s built-in listing feature to automatically display all notes in the notes/ directory. New notes are picked up automatically, with no need to manually update the home page.



To create a new note, copy notes/_template.md and fill in the YAML header. See the project README for full instructions.




This is a demo note showing categories and the listing page.
See demo-features for a demo of wikilinks and citations.

title: Graph View
My Notes
text:
Welcome to My Notes, rendered from an Obsidian vault using Quarto.

Browse all notes on the Notes page
Explore connections in the Graph visualisation







Note



These are demo notes that showcase template features. Replace them with your own content.

title: Demo Features
text:
This demo note shows how wikilinks and citations work in this project.



Obsidian-style [[wikilinks]] are automatically converted to standard links at render time by a Lua filter (scripts/wikilinks.lua). Source files are never modified.
Plain wikilink: Link to another note using [[note-filename]] syntax, for example, [[hello-world]].
Aliased wikilink: Use [[target|display text]] to customise the link text, for example, [[hello-world|the other demo note]].



Citations use Pandoc’s @citekey syntax, referencing entries in references.bib. For example, (1) is a fictitious reference used here purely to show how inline citations render. Multiple citations can be grouped (1,2).




This is a demo note showing wikilinks and citations.
See hello-world for another feature demo.






1. Demo AB, Example CD. A fictitious article for demonstration purposes only. Journal of Placeholder Studies 2025;1(1). :1–10. doi: 10.1234/fake-doi-demo-2025


2. Example CD, Test EF. Another fictitious article used to demonstrate citation rendering. Annals of Dummy Research 2024;42(3). :100–105. doi: 10.1234/fake-doi-example-2024

title: Demo Features
text:
Obsidian-style [[wikilinks]] are automatically converted to standard links at render time by a Lua filter (scripts/wikilinks.lua). Source files are never modified.
Plain wikilink: Link to another note using [[note-filename]] syntax, for example, [[hello-world]].
Aliased wikilink: Use [[target|display text]] to customise the link text, for example, [[hello-world|the other demo note]].

title: Demo Features
